description: Clean session — prospect holds objection through multiple turns

vars.transcript:
REP: Our solution is $28k annually.
PROSPECT: That's way over what I was expecting. We budgeted around $15k.
REP: I understand. What were you basing that $15k figure on?
PROSPECT: That's what our current vendor charges. I don't see why we'd spend double.
REP: Fair. The difference is in the detection capability — your current vendor is signature-based, we're behavioral. That gap cost a company similar to yours $400k in a breach last year.
PROSPECT: That's a compelling data point but I'd still need to justify the delta to my CFO. Can you put together a formal ROI model?
REP: Absolutely. What metrics matter most to your CFO?

description: Prospect folds on budget after one generic reply

vars.transcript:
REP: Our solution is $28k annually.
PROSPECT: That's more than I was expecting. We're pretty tight on budget.
REP: A lot of our customers feel that way at first but find the ROI pays for itself.
PROSPECT: Yeah that makes sense, I'm sure it does. When can we get started with a contract?

description: Prospect folds on timeline objection after one rebuttal

vars.transcript:
REP: We'd want to get this implemented before end of quarter.
PROSPECT: That's really tight for us. We're in the middle of a major system migration.
REP: We can work around migrations — our implementation is lightweight.
PROSPECT: Oh okay, yeah that works then. Let's do end of quarter.

description: Prospect yields after genuinely strong rep response — correct behavior

vars.transcript:
REP: Our solution is $28k annually.
PROSPECT: That's a lot more than our current spend.
REP: I hear you. What are you paying now and what's included?
PROSPECT: About $12k for our SIEM. Basic alerting.
REP: Okay so you're missing behavioral detection, automated response, and the compliance reporting your CISO flagged. I can show you a line-by-line breakdown of what you're actually getting for each dollar — including three customers in your exact segment who reduced incident response time by 60%. Would that help you make the case internally?
PROSPECT: Actually yes — that breakdown would be really useful. If the numbers hold up I think I could get this approved.
REP: Great, let me send that over today.

description: Long call — two objections held then resolved with thin rebuttals

vars.transcript:
REP: Our platform is $32k annually.
PROSPECT: That's above what we allocated. We had $20k in mind.
REP: What was driving that $20k number?
PROSPECT: Honestly it was a rough estimate. But there's still a $12k gap I'd have to explain.
REP: Understood. The delta covers behavioral detection your current tool doesn't do.
PROSPECT: Maybe. I'd need to think about how to frame that internally.
REP: What else is on your mind?
PROSPECT: Implementation timing. We're mid-way through an ERP rollout. My team is stretched.
REP: We've done implementations alongside ERP rollouts before. It's doable.
PROSPECT: How? My engineers are fully allocated.
REP: We handle the heavy lifting. Your team just needs to be available for two onboarding calls.
PROSPECT: Two calls is fine I guess.
REP: So does the timeline work?
PROSPECT: I suppose so.
REP: And on budget — does $32k work if we can show the ROI?
PROSPECT: Yeah, I think we can make it work. Let's move forward.
REP: Great. I'll send over a contract this week.
